West Virginia Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
Compact Enacted - Not Yet Active

Reciprocity in West Virginia for Dietitians

West Virginia has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. Until implementation goes live, apply by endorsement through the state board.

Once active, compact participation should reduce or eliminate the need for a separate transfer application.

Endorsement fee
$69
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of board-approved supervised dietetic experience.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies this.

  3. Submit the Application to WVBOLD

    Download and complete the application form from the WVBOLD website. Submit it with the $69 fee.

  4. Provide Verification Documents

    Submit a copy of your active CDR card or have verification sent directly to the board. Ensure you meet the age (18+) and citizenship or legal alien requirements.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$69
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$69
Renewal cycle
Annual + 20 CE hours every two years

Required Documents

  • Official Application: Application form filled out accurately and completely, confirming age and citizenship.
  • CDR Registration Card: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official College Transcripts: Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree (may be satisfied by CDR card, check with board).

Board Contact

Board
West Virginia Board of Licensed Dietitians (WVBOLD)
Department
Independent State Board
Phone
(304) 558-1024
Email
wvbold@wv.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $69. Renewal cycle: Annual + 20 CE hours every two years.

West Virginia Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a West Virginia dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $69. A provisional permit application costs $46.

When does the West Virginia license need to be renewed?

West Virginia dietitian licenses are renewed annually. The renewal fee is $69.

What are the continuing education requirements for West Virginia?

To maintain licensure, West Virginia requires 20 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) every two years.

Does West Virginia have reciprocity for dietitians?

West Virginia has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to West Virginia?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
